title: 'Politicisation by Pakistan': Lalit Modi blames PCB for India-Pak cricket freeze
content: Former IPL chairman Lalit Modi expressed pessimism about India and Pakistan resuming bilateral cricket soon, citing significant political interference within the Pakistan Cricket Board as the primary obstacle. He believes external pressures have politicized decisions, hindering any possibility of a series, even at neutral venues, and fears the situation will worsen.

title: 'Cried day before pre-boards': CBSE Class 10 topper Gunnika Khurana turned anxiety into success
content: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) declared the Class 10th result on April 15, 2026, a day that once again brought the familiar rush of anxiety, anticipation, and constantly refreshing screens. Among the toppers was Gunnika Khurana, who secured 99.6% and spoke about a journey shaped more by consistency and calm than pressure. From early preparation in grade nine to moments of pre-board anxiety, her story reflects discipline, self-belief, and quiet resilience behind a near-perfect score.

title: How change in H-1B selection system has been a boon to employees already in the US
content: US businesses are witnessing significantly higher H-1B visa selection rates, with some reporting over 50% success. This surge follows the Department of Homeland Security's shift from a random lottery to a wage-weighted system, prioritizing higher salary offers. The new rule, effective February 27, 2026, aims to reshape the selection process for skilled foreign workers.

title: Jharkhand JAC Class 8 result 2026 released: Direct link to download scorecards here
content: Jharkhand Academic Council has announced Class 8 results for 2026. Students can now access their scorecards online by entering their roll code and number on the official websites. The scorecard contains vital details like subject-wise marks and qualifying status. It is advised to verify all information and save a copy for future reference.

title: Did Pete Hegseth quote Pulp Fiction verse at prayer meet in Pentagon?
content: At a Pentagon worship service, US Secretary of War Pete Hegseth recited what he called “CSAR 25:17,” presenting it as a military prayer tied to combat search-and-rescue missions. He suggested it was meant to reflect Ezekiel 25:17, which is where the confusion begins.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' box office collection day 29: Ranveer Singh film marches past Rs 1,100 cr
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' has officially breached the Rs. 1,100 crore net milestone in India on Day 29. From its massive Rs. 90 crore opening day to becoming the first Hindi film to enter the Rs. 1,000 crore club, Aditya Dhar’s spy thriller starring Ranveer Singh is rewriting history. Read on to learn more about the latest live tracking updates, day-wise collection breakdown for Week 1, 2, and 3 and a recap of the worldwide gross hitting Rs. 1,733 crore despite the IPL 2026 craze.

title: No jet fuel in 6 weeks: Europe may see mass flight cancellations soon
content: Europe faces a critical jet fuel shortage, potentially lasting only six weeks, warns the International Energy Agency chief.  Disruptions from the Iran conflict could soon ground flights.  This "largest energy crisis" threatens global economic growth and inflation, with developing nations in Asia and Africa bearing the brunt before impacting Europe and the Americas.

title: Everyone says TPUs are killing Nvidia, but Jensen Huang just explained why Nvidia wins anyway
content: Jensen Huang pushed back hard on the idea that Google's TPUs, Meta's MTIA chips, Anthropic's multi-gigawatt compute deal, Amazon's Trainium4, and OpenAI's custom Broadcom silicon spell the beginning of the end for Nvidia. Speaking on the Dwarkesh Podcast, the Nvidia CEO called Anthropic's TPU pivot a one-off, defended CUDA's staying power, and conceded one early mistake—while largely sidestepping the inference era's uncomfortable cost math.

title: 'My guarantee': PM Modi says no state to face injustice due to delimitation
content: The Congress on Thursday alleged that the government wants to "bulldoze" delimitation in the name of women's reservation, and asserted that if it is really committed to implementing the women's quota law it should do so immediately on the basis of the current strength of Lok Sabha. The opposition party also alleged that the BJP was using delimitation as a "political weapon" to increase its influence.

title: IPL 2026: 4 players who could replace injured Khaleel Ahmed in CSK
content: Chennai Super Kings face a major blow as Khaleel Ahmed is out of IPL 2026 with a quadriceps injury. His absence comes at a crucial time as CSK seeks to build on recent momentum. The team is now exploring replacements like Chetan Sakariya, Akash Madhwal, Simarjeet Singh, and Kamlesh Nagarkoti to bolster their bowling attack.

title: LeT co-founder Amir Hamza shot at in Lahore in second attack within a year
content: Lashkar-e-Taiba co-founder Amir Hamza was shot by unknown gunmen in Lahore, marking a second assassination attempt in less than a year. The 67-year-old, a designated global terrorist, was attacked outside a TV station while accompanied by a Pakistani judge. This incident follows a previous attempt where he survived a shooting last May.

title: BSF officer killed in fuel depot fire at Bengal border outpost
content: Tragedy struck the Bankra border outpost in West Bengal's North 24 Parganas as a devastating fire engulfed a fuel depot. Assistant Sub Inspector Jagi Ram tragically lost his life after being trapped inside during a blast that ignited the blaze. The depot supplied fuel for BSF boats patrolling the Sundarbans. Authorities are investigating the incident.

title: MI vs PBKS: Shreyas Iyer to Jasprit Bumrah - 5 key player battles to watch
content: Mumbai Indians face Punjab Kings in a crucial IPL 2026 clash at Wankhede Stadium. MI, struggling with three consecutive losses and a doubtful Rohit Sharma, host an unbeaten PBKS. This historic rivalry is evenly matched, with key individual battles set to decide the outcome.

title: Exclusive: Jubin Nautiyal ties the knot with childhood sweetheart
content: Singer Jubin Nautiyal has married his childhood sweetheart. The wedding was a private affair with only close family present. Nautiyal chose a simple, traditional ceremony over a large public celebration. He has always kept his personal life private. Nautiyal had previously shared a glimpse of his partner on Valentine's Day. The singer has not commented on the news.

title: 'Don't want credit': Top quotes from PM Modi's Lok Sabha speech on women's reservation bill
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi hailed the women's reservation Bill as a defining moment for India's democracy, emphasizing the need for greater female representation in policymaking. He asserted that the Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam, providing 33% reservation for women, will give India a new direction, linking it to the nation's ancient democratic roots and the vision of a 'Viksit Bharat'.

title: Newborns left in dustbins, buses, forests: MP tops infant abandonment for 5th straight year
content: Madhya Pradesh leads the nation in infant abandonment cases. Shocking incidents reveal newborns left in dangerous situations, some surviving miraculously. The National Crime Records Bureau highlights this ongoing crisis. Fear, social stigma, and policy pressures drive these extreme acts. Addressing this requires urgent awareness and support systems.

title: 'Dosa, idly' row: From Jitesh's clip to DJ — what sparked CSK vs RCB controversy
content: In the complaint, CSK said the playing of the track "Dosa, idli, sambar, chutney, chutney" during the match was "not in good taste." The song is often used in memes linked to South Indian stereotypes.

title: 'World’s largest water fight': 191 killed in 3 days of Thailand’s Songkran festival
content: Thailand's Songkran festival, known for its water fights, has seen a tragic start with at least 191 road accident fatalities in the first three days. Speeding and drunk driving were the primary causes, despite government safety efforts. This surge highlights the ongoing road safety challenges during the "seven dangerous days" of the festival.

title: New route? How are Iran-linked ships passing through Strait of Hormuz despite US blockade
content: Amidst a US naval blockade, Iran-linked vessels navigated the Strait of Hormuz, signalling potential route shifts.  Traffic remains significantly reduced, with heightened checks by both US and Iranian forces slowing crossings. Conflicting claims emerge regarding successful breaches, as electronic interference and disabled tracking systems obscure full visibility in this critical global oil transit zone.

title: 'Religion-based reservation unconstitutional': Amit Shah & Akhilesh Yadav clash over delimitation
content: Home Minister Amit Shah and SP chief Akhilesh Yadav clashed in Parliament over the Women's Reservation Bill. Yadav questioned the urgency without an updated Census, demanding a caste-based enumeration. Shah assured that a caste census would be conducted after the ongoing Census process, while also stating religion-based reservations are unconstitutional.

title: Amid TCS Nashik row, Tech Mahindra denies religious bias allegations at Mumbai’s Goregaon office
content: Tech Mahindra denies allegations of religious bias at its Goregaon office after social media claims surfaced. This follows heightened scrutiny on IT firms, with TCS Nashik facing a police probe over sexual harassment and religious conversion allegations. Nine FIRs have been registered at TCS Nashik, leading to several arrests.

title: Parents in Portugal, 5-year-old tied to iron gate by cop grandmother; police dept acts
content: An assistant sub-inspector in Faridkot was suspended after allegedly tying her five-year-old granddaughter to a gate. Neighbors intervened after hearing the child wailing and found her hands and feet bound. The ASI, who was caring for the child while her parents were abroad, was suspended after a video of the incident went viral.

title: 'Serial bride & fake dad' flee with grooms' cash, gold near Mumbai
content: Kashimira police apprehended a man and woman for a wedding scam, where the bride would abscond with valuables days after the ceremony.  The duo, targeting divorced men over 40, were involved in five Maharashtra cases.  The man posed as family to arrange marriages, with the woman then fleeing with cash and jewellery.  They have been remanded in custody.

title: Hormuz disruptions hit! LPG imports halve from Feb levels, domestic output also impacted
content: Ongoing disruptions to energy flows from the Gulf linked to the US-Iran war continue to unsettle global oil and gas markets, and a ceasefire in place for about a week has done little to improve the availability of cooking gas.

title: Noida workers protest: UP govt hikes minimum wages, but can exporters afford it?
content: Garment exporters in Uttar Pradesh face a dilemma as a recent wage hike for workers clashes with rising input costs and squeezed margins.  Ongoing labor unrest in Noida factories is causing concern among global buyers, who are seeking clarity on potential shipment delays and may shift orders to competing nations.

title: HAL’s Light Utility Helicopter: Specs, altitude edge and why it matters for India’s Siachen ops
content: In the super high altitude border areas in both the North and the North East, the Cheetah helicopter is not just a machine, its a lifeline for the troops deployed on desolate posts in defence of India. In these areas the helicopter is a lifeline and often the sole mode of transport. As the Chetak and the Cheetah helicopter head towards retirement, a new helicopter India's own Light Utility Helicopter is poised to take their place.

title: 'Saari nazar utaar denge': PSL CEO wards off 'evil eye' for Babar Azam - Watch
content: The clip surfaced after Zalmi’s match in the ongoing 11th edition of the Pakistan Super League and showed a light exchange during a public interaction. "Aaj saari nazar utaar denge," a person is heard saying in the video.

title: Dismissed UP cop kills beggar to fake his own death, burns body, leaves ID near remains
content: A former Uttar Pradesh Police constable has been arrested in Hathras for allegedly murdering a beggar. The accused then burned the beggar's body and placed his own belongings nearby to fake his death. Police uncovered the elaborate plot during their investigation. The dismissed constable, Ramveer Singh, has a history of serious criminal charges across multiple districts.

title: Women’s Reservation Bill: How numbers stack up in Lok Sabha and what delimitation could change
content: Parliament is set to debate the Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam, aiming for 33% women's reservation in legislatures. This constitutional amendment, passed in 2023, will be implemented after delimitation based on the 2027 Census. While the government champions women's empowerment, opposition raises concerns over the delimitation process and its potential impact on state representation, creating a north-south divide.

title: How this teenager survived a plane crash - injured, alone and in harsh land
content: At 17, Juliane Koepcke survived a plane crash, falling 3,000 meters into the Amazon rainforest. She then spent 11 days navigating the jungle, a place she knew from her childhood, before being found by fishermen. Her survival, marked by resilience and familiarity with her surroundings, remains a powerful testament to endurance.

title: New record! Apple’s India vendors export $2.5 bn components to China; may reach $3.5 bn
content: Several Apple suppliers in India contributed to shipments to China, including Foxconn, Tata Electronics, Tata-owned Pegatron Technology India, Motherson, Salcomp, TRIL Bangalore and Yuzhan Technology.

title: 'Trying to get a little breathing room': Trump says Israel-Lebanon talks to be on Thursday
content: President Trump announced that Israel and Lebanon are set to hold peace talks, aiming to ease long-standing tensions. This follows a historic trilateral meeting hosted by the US, where both nations expressed willingness for direct negotiations, a ceasefire, and a broader peace framework.

title: Touching moment: Kohli stunned as LSG youngster tries to touch his feet - Watch
content: During an interaction after the match, Tiwari bent down to touch Kohli's feet, a gesture used to show respect. Kohli appeared taken aback and responded with a smile. The video has gone viral on social media.

title: CBSE Class 10 result 2026: How did Delhi perform in this year’s board examinations?
content: CBSE has declared the Class 10 results 2026 with a 93.70% pass rate, showing a slight rise over last year and stable national performance. Delhi outperformed the average with 97.39%, led by strong results in East and West regions. Low compartment rates and high participation underline consistent outcomes despite ongoing exam reforms.

title: Kawhi mum on future, 'not stressing' NBA probe
content: Kawhi Leonard declined to discuss his future with the Clippers after Wednesday's play-in loss and said he's "not stressing" the NBA's investigation into his deal with Aspiration.

title: Tiger fighting subpoena for prescription records
content: Tiger Woods' attorney is fighting prosecutors' attempts to subpoena the 15-time major champion's prescription drug records from a pharmacy, according to records.

title: Vintage Curry takes over as Dubs extend season
content: The Warriors, led by Stephen Curry, erased a 13-point deficit in the fourth quarter to edge the Clippers and advance in the NBA's play-in tournament.

title: LIV CEO says season to continue 'at full throttle'
content: Amid reports that Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund might be on the verge of pulling its funding for the league, LIV Golf CEO Scott O'Neil told staff in an email Wednesday that its season will go on "as planned, uninterrupted and at full throttle."

title: Ulberg says he lost UFC title belt celebrating win
content: Newly crowned UFC light heavyweight champion Carlos Ulberg told Fox Sports Australia that he misplaced his title belt while celebrating his victory.

title: Minus Embiid, Maxey propels Sixers into playoffs
content: With Joel Embiid watching from the bench, Tyrese Maxey scored 31 points to lead the Sixers past the Magic 109-97 and lock up a first-round playoff date with the rival Boston Celtics.

title: Sources: MLBPA fires two top execs with cause
content: The Major League Baseball Players Association fired two top executives with cause Wednesday following an internal investigation, sources told ESPN.

title: Hornets' Ball fined $35K for 'reckless' Bam play
content: The NBA has fined LaMelo Ball $35,000 "for making unnecessary and reckless contact" with Bam Adebayo during Tuesday's play-in game. The Hornets star also was fined $25,000 for using profane language during a TV interview after that game.

title: 🏈 Kiper's two-round NFL mock draft
content: Mel Kiper Jr. predicts 64 picks across the first two rounds of the draft, including two potential trades, a few QB landing spots and plenty of impact additions.

title: How every NFL team can ace the draft: Solak plans perfect moves, picks for all 32 rosters
content: A week before the NFL draft, Ben Solak hits the top priorities for every single team -- and the prospects who can meet those needs.

title: How a 'big argument' and lucky draft moves led to the Chiefs selecting Chris Jones
content: A look back 10 years later at the selection of Jones, one of the most important picks in franchise history.

title: WrestleMania 42: Key stats for all 13 matches across both nights
content: With eight title bouts in two nights, WrestleMania 42 is stacked. Get caught up before this weekend's PLE.

title: How 48 hours changed the Lakers' season
content: The Lakers lost two of their three stars back on April 2. Now they're a home underdog against the Rockets.

title: Stanley Cup contender flaws: Biggest issues for the NHL's top playoff teams
content: No NHL team is perfect, and postseason opponents will be certain to key in on these areas of weakness.

title: Playoff watch: What's at stake on the season's final day
content: There are six games left to finalize playoff seeding and draft lottery order. Here's a rundown of what's on tap.

title: UCL talking points: Slot's bizarre choices, Bayern now favorites?
content: Arne Slot's decision to start Alexander Isak backfired, badly. ESPN's Julien Laurens, Sam Marsden, Gab Marcotti and Sam Tighe weigh in on that and all the other moments that mattered from the UCL quarterfinal second legs.

title: Inside the making of Mariners' now-famous Ichiro Suzuki statue
content: You know about the reveal mishap. Here's what went into creating the sculpture that will forever honor a Seattle icon.

title: MLB Power Rankings: NL teams dominate top 10 with two debuts
content: Three weeks into the season, two red-hot NL teams are shooting up our list while another powerhouse plummets.

title: What makes Arizona QB Noah Fifita tick? Loyalty, generosity
content: Fifita, who runs monthly community events, is one of just five senior QBs nationally who has not transferred.

title: More than a jersey: Kits are putting soccer's underdogs on the map
content: Few lower-league clubs could ever take on soccer's giants, but when it comes to attention-grabbing kits, these three are in a league of their own.

title: Can Jordan Chiles end her career with an NCAA team title?
content: Jordan Chiles enters her final NCAA championships as the most recognizable face in gymnastics -- and hopes to lead UCLA to its first title since 2018.

title: 'No excuses': How LSU's strength coach became the ultimate example of fortitude
content: In July, a Jet Ski accident caused the amputation of Katie Guillory's foot. But it only strengthened her drive.

title: Men's transfer portal: Tracking who's on the move, commitments
content: Tracking news and updates for players entering -- and exiting -- the transfer portal for the 2026-27 season.

title: LeBron outside the top 20?! Steph at 50?! How we ranked this postseason's stars
content: ESPN analyst Zach Kram ranked and tiered the 50 NBA players most likely to make the biggest impacts in the postseason.

title: WNBA free agency and trade tracker: Aces' Wilson signs 3-year, $5M deal
content: WNBA free agency is here. Kelsey Plum is returning to Los Angeles. Kayla McBride is returning to Minnesota.

title: Alycia Baumgardner's journey to become the face of women's boxing
content: An early setback and a positive test -- since cleared -- didn't define Baumgardner. Now, she's on the doorstep of leading the sport.

title: MMA pound-for-pound rankings: Suarez seizes control of a spot in women's top 10
content: Onetime UFC strawweight title challenger secures her spot in the ESPN P4P rankings with dominant wrestling.

title: Mason Miller tops all pitchers? CJ Abrams goes 30-100-30? Don't be surprised
content: Eric Karabell looks into the crystal ball to tell fantasy baseball managers what might come to pass in the not-too-distant future.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Newcastle to bring Mourinho back to Premier League?
content: Newcastle United could sack manager Eddie Howe and turn to Jose Mourinho. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: BJP MP Tejasvi Surya’s remarks trigger sharp reactions; Telangana Congress, BRS leaders demand apology
content: Telangana Transport Minister terms the remarks made in the Parliament as “an attack on the self-respect of Telangana people” and “a distortion of history”

title: No State, be it north or south will be discriminated against in delimitation exercise: PM in LS
content: The Prime Minister said no one should remain under the illusion that they are giving something to the women of this country as reservation in legislatures is their right

title: Developed India can only be built on foundation of its native languages, says President
content: Speaking at the sixth convocation of the Mahatma Gandhi International Hindi University in Maharashtra’s Wardha, Droupadi Murmu urged every Indian to learn at least one more Indian language besides their local one

title: Academics raise concerns over internship component of FYUGP in Calicut University
content: University has not put in place adequate mechanisms to support students in securing suitable and meaningful internship opportunities, say Senate members of Congress-aligned Kerala Private College Teachers’ Association

title: Kerala dental student’s death: College to dismiss faculty member as protests intensify
content: Management’s decision to initiate dismissal proceedings comes after discussions with student union representatives. Students had earlier warned of class boycott until action was taken against the faculty member

title: Supreme Court says time is now too short to look into ‘mass transfer’ of high-ranking West Bengal officials ahead of election
content: The petition challenges the ECI’s decision to transfer ‘wholesale’ 63 IPS officers and 16 senior bureaucrats, including the Director General of Police and the Chief Secretary, in the ‘garb of poll-preparedness’

title: Operation Numkhor to figure in Indo-Bhutan Customs meeting
content: Operation Numkhor has emerged as one of the most extensive crackdowns on vehicle smuggling and forged registrations. Statewide raids were launched in Kerala in Sept. 2025

title: Assembly Elections 2026 LIVE: TVK releases its election manifesto; Vijay announces ₹2,500 monthly assistance, 8 gm gold for women
content: PM Modi urged people to come together to build a state where "the poor have shelter, food security is ensured, women feel safe, and youth can find jobs without leaving the state."

title: Bills to amend women’s reservation law, and carry out fresh delimitation, introduced after division of vote in Lok Sabha
content: The motion was carried with 251 members voting in favour and 185 against; Opposition members accused the government of using the amendment to push ahead with delimitation rather than to implement the women’s quota

title: 2.5-acre parking facility at Kempegowda Bus Station Terminal-1 in Bengaluru inaugurated
content: Dedicated parking has been allocated for buses of the North Western and Kalyana Karnataka Road Transport Corporations, which will improve operational efficiency and reduce congestion within the terminal

title: Madras High Court dismisses Tamannaah Bhatia’s plea for ₹1-crore damages from Power Soaps
content: She had accused the company of having exploited her popularity even after the expiry of the agreement period, but failed to prove it

title: Cleanliness, safety, housing top agenda of many women candidates contesting in Chennai
content: NTK’s Ayesha says traffic congestion has to be addressed immediately in Triplicane; AIADMK’s Gokula Indira seeks increased patrol for women’s safety and designated spaces for food carts

title: Mathura boat capsize: Last pilgrim's missing body found, death toll climbs to 16
content: Police said that with the latest recovery, all pilgrims who had gone missing after the incident have been accounted for

title: Delimitation Bill will not affect Tamil Nadu: AIADMK leader Edappadi Palaniswami
content: When Union Minister Amit Shah visited Tamil Nadu, he assured that the State will not be affected, stated Mr. Palaniswami. “The NDA will implement delimitation better than what Mr. Stalin expects. The Centre is clear that no State will be affected by the delimitation,” he added.

title: 2-km-long queue for auto LPG in Bengaluru, claim drivers
content: Drivers say the situation had improved slightly following recent protests, but has deteriorated again over the past two days

title: Delhi Speaker gets Z-category security after Assembly gets repeated bomb threats
content: The move comes after the series of targeted bomb threats and a recent high-profile security breach, the Delhi Assembly Secretariat said.

title: ‘Oru Durooha Saahacharyathil’ movie review: Falters after a glorious opening hour
content: Despite fine performances from Kunchacko Boban and Dileesh Pothan, ‘Oru Durooha Saahacharyathil’ loses rhythm in the last act as the director trades well-written humour and heartfelt moments for shock value

title: Peak electricity demand crosses 6,000 MW for the first time in Kerala
content: The Kerala State Electricity Board will review power position on Friday. With summer rainfall almost nil, the KSEB has been keeping internal hydel generation to a minimum

title: SC seeks govt. response on plea challenging Shariat inheritance as discriminatory to women
content: The petition said Muslim women remain the only substantial class of women in the country who have been denied equal inheritance rights

title: Krantiveera Sangolli Rayanna Urban Co-operative Society fraud case: Those duped by film producer told to submit relevant documents in Bengaluru
content: As per the notice from the competent authority, depositors must submit 10 types of documents to process their refund claims

title: Woman ‘killed, eaten’ by unidentified animal in Nilgiris
content: The incident is likely to have occurred on April 11, but her remains were discovered on Wednesday; Hair samples from the scene have been collected to identify the animal species

title: Karnataka Congress MLAs meet Randeep Singh Surjewala in Delhi, seek Cabinet berths
content: They want the CM to accommodate senior legislators by replacing those who have held ministerial positions multiple times

title: Camera traps set up to identify animal responsible for woman’s death in Nilgiris
content: Forest department officials dismissed claims that the animal responsible for the attack is the same tiger that was responsible for the killing of two Toda adivasi men in Governor Shola in 2025 and Sholur in 2026

title: How is a Constitution Amendment Bill passed?
content: A Constitutional Amendment Bill must secure two-thirds of majority votes in both Houses, and get ratified by at least 50% State legislatures, if it affects the federal structure, before receiving an assent from President

title: Death of BDS student: MSF leaders arrested after clash with police
content: As the leaders attempted to meet the college Principal for discussions, the police blocked their entry, triggering a confrontation

title: Ten labourers injured as auto overturns in Nagayalanka, four in critical condition
content: Ten workers from Ganapeswaram village in Krishna district were injured when an auto overturned near Sai Baba temple in Nagayalanka on Thursday

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: Distribution of booth slips underway
content: The Voter Information Slip contains voter’s details, location of the polling station, date and time of polling and a snapshot of the polling station map along with a list of Do’s and Don’ts to guide the voters on poll day, an official release said.

title: Why the TCS Nashik case has sparked outrage | Pulse Maharashtra
content: A major police investigation is underway into allegations involving employees at Tata Consultancy Services’ Nashik BPO facility. Multiple FIRs have been registered accusing certain staff members of sexual harassment, coercion, and attempts at religious conversion. As arrests are made and an SIT probes the claims, the case has triggered outrage and raised wider questions about workplace safety, consent, religious freedom, and due process. TCS has suspended the accused employees and launched an internal inquiry as the investigation continues.

title: Illegal cigarette sale rackets busted in Bengaluru; goods worth ₹45 lakh seized
content: In one of the cases, the accused revealed that the cigarettes were being sourced from a Delhi-based supplier

title: Congress risks alienating AHINDA community in Karnataka for the sake of one family, warn Muslim clerics
content: Say the Congress had acted in violation of norms in taking action against certain leaders in connection with the Davanagere South byelection.

title: Allahabad High Court flags lodging of ‘fake FIRs’ under anti-conversion law in Uttar Pradesh
content: The Bench stated that it has observed a ‘troubling pattern of misuse’ of the law by the police and third-party complainants

title: India-Zambia talks on critical minerals stall over mining rights: sources
content: “New Delhi is making efforts to restart discussions with Zambia, ‌but the situation is still uncertain,” one ​of the sources said

title: PLHIV can have healthy babies (HIV Negative); NGO holds baby shower to create awareness
content: Homoeopathic Academy of Research and Charities has worked for the health care and rehabilitation of HIV-infected orphans for the past several years

title: All States will have their number of Lok Sabha seats increase by 50% after delimitation: Official
content: No State will lose current proportional strength in Parliament, says govt. functionary; while not mentioned in the draft Bills, Home Minister has assured representatives of increase, official adds

title: Eight pilgrims from Chikkamagaluru killed, 12 hurt as vehicle rams truck near Mantralayam in Kurnool district
content: Nearly 20 persons from Chikkamagaluru district in neighbouring Karnataka were on their way to Sri Raghavendra Swamy Mutt, Mantralayam

title: Pawan Kalyan welcomes Nari Shakti Vandan Amendment Bill for 33% women’s reservation
content: Pawan Kalyan welcomes introduction of Nari Shakti Vandan Amendment Bill, 2026 in Lok Sabha, backing 33% women’s reservation

title: Congress suspends 5 Haryana MLAs for cross-voting in Rajya Sabha polls
content: Earlier, the Congress issued a show cause notice to its five legislators from Haryana for alleged cross-voting

title: Woman ASI in Punjab's Faridkot suspended for tying 5-year-old granddaughter and leaving her in scorching heat
content: Sarabjit Kaur, ASI in Punjab’s Faridkot, tied her granddaughter to a gate and left her in the heat, sparking outrage

title: Kolleru Lake faces fresh encroachment; Jal Biradari seeks Supreme Court order enforcement
content: Jal Biradari National Convenor Bolisetty Satyanarayana says 1.61 lakh acres of Kolleru Lake are encroached, and urges the AP government to act on fresh violations

title: Ahead of Assembly election results, Congress leadership debate in Kerala heats up
content: In a contentious FB post, K. Sudhakaran backs K.C. Venugopal. While Ramesh Chennithala says party high command will take final call, KPCC president Sunny Joseph points out ‘senior leaders should not jump the gun’ on the matter. Left supporters seek to characterise ‘feuding’ in Congress as a move aimed at testing ‘factional loyalties and flushing out potential rivals’

title: Cases filed against DMK, AIADMK candidates for alleged distribution of ₹8,000 coupons and ₹10,000 tokens
content: Madras High Court Chief Justice Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari and Justice G. Arul Murugan agree to hear both the cases on Friday

title: Relief from heatwave in Telangana likely as IMD forecasts rain between April 19-22
content: A low-pressure system moving towards Telangana is creating favourable conditions for rainfall, says IMD-Hyderabad’s Scientist Dharma Raju

title: Key aspects in Telangana’s Caste Census-2024 four volumes and Expert Group’s reports
content: Broadly, the four volumes has data on percentage of people — across social categories — who benefitted from reservations in various categories of education and government employment; livelihoods, education levels and other key aspects.

title: Supreme Court refuses to interfere with Bombay HC verdict lifting stay on fraud proceedings against Anil Ambani
content: A Bench headed by CJI Surya Kant did not intervene against the Division Bench decision to set aside a Single Judge order of December last year

title: Explained | Assam’s delimitation test
content: What is the Election Commission’s draft proposal on delimitation? Why are people opposing it? Who is going to get affected by the exercise? Why do some parties want the delimitation done after the National Register of Citizens is updated? Is there a possibility of the proposal being redrafted?

title: Migrants Are Faking Domestic Abuse To Fast-Track UK Permanent Residency
content: Under the current home rules, migrants on temporary visas who have a British partner can apply for special concessions by claiming domestic abuse.

title: Parliament Special Session LIVE: "50% Of Country's Population Should Be Made Part Of Decision-Making": PM Modi
content: Parliament's 3-day special session Live Updates, Day 1: The government is expected to introduce three amendment Bills aimed at operationalising the legislation, which was passed in 2023 to provide 33% reservation for women in the Lok Sabha and State

title: "My Duty To Take Everyone Along": PM To Samajwadi Party MP On OBC Remark
content: Dharmendra Yadav alleged that PM Modi is from the OBC community but does not keep their interest in mind.

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Pakistan Army Chief Meets Iran Speaker Ghalibaf

title: Can Iran Legally Collect Tolls On Vessels Passing Strait Of Hormuz?
content: UN Convention on the Law of the Sea provides vessels a right of unimpeded "transit passage" through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Assam delimitation proposal: an erosion of representation

title: How Tamil Nadu got 234 Assembly seats: The Delimitation Commission’s calculated departure
content: Under Article 170 of the Constitution and Section 8(b) of the Delimitation Commission Act, 1962, the number of seats in a State Assembly must be an integral multiple of the number of Lok Sabha seats allotted to that State
